## reference · ux onboarding tutorials first run skip

* Tutorials during first app launch are often **skipped by users** and easily forgotten.
* **Push revelations** (intrusive tutorials) interrupt users, slow them down, and don't improve task performance.
* Users want to **start using the product immediately**, not spend time studying how to use it.
* Guidance provided by tutorials is **hard to remember** when needed, as it's shown out of context.
* **Pull revelations** (contextual help) can solve these issues by presenting help content contextually.
* To implement pull revelations:
	+ Make help content easy to dismiss and recall.
	+ Use progressive disclosure in help content.
	+ Avoid requiring memorization for multistep workflows.
	+ Skip obvious information and focus on complex functionality.
	+ Understand the user's journey to a feature to provide relevant help.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/onboarding-tutorials/
